message:
feat(core): migrate caches to bounded LRU, Arc-wrap version store, harden snapshot shipping

Connection parse and compiled caches (connection.rs):
- Replace unbounded HashMap caches with LRU caches (lru crate v0.12)
  bounded at 256 entries (parse) and 128 entries (compiled). The previous
  implementation used HashMap::clear() as a blunt eviction when capacity
  was exceeded, which caused periodic cache thrashing. LRU eviction
  preserves hot entries and provides O(1) amortized insert/lookup.
- Wrap ParseCacheEntry and CompiledCacheEntry in Arc so cache lookups
  return cheap clones of the entry pointer instead of deep-cloning AST
  trees and VdbeProgram bytecode on every cache hit.
- Change lookup methods to return Arc<...Entry> and take &mut borrow
  (required by LRU's get() which updates recency).
- insert_parse_cache and insert_compiled_cache now return Arc<Entry> so
  the caller can use the entry immediately without a second lookup.
- invalidate_compiled_cache uses peek() (no recency update) + pop()
  instead of get() + remove().
- Statement and VdbeProgram fields inside cache entries wrapped in Arc
  for zero-copy sharing with callers.

Version store thread-safety (connection.rs):
- Migrate version_store from Rc<RefCell<VersionStore>> to Arc<VersionStore>,
  preparing for cross-thread snapshot reads. GC calls now go through
  Arc directly instead of borrow().

FK action type path fix (connection.rs):
- Foreign key ON UPDATE/DELETE action matching switched from
  fsqlite_ast::FkActionType to fsqlite_vdbe::codegen::FkActionType and
  fsqlite_vdbe::codegen::FkDef, aligning with the actual type used in
  the schema after codegen processing.

Minor cleanups (connection.rs):
- Replace `if let Err(e) = x { return Err(e) }` with idiomatic `x?`
  in two commit paths.
- Replace `if let Ok(()) = commit_res` with `if matches!(commit_res, Ok(()))`
  to avoid the clippy-flagged binding pattern.
- Fix trailing whitespace in FK-related blocks.

Snapshot shipping hardening (snapshot_shipping.rs):
- Add overflow-safe multiplication check (checked_mul) when computing
  required changeset data length from n_pages x entry_size. Returns
  DatabaseCorrupt on overflow instead of panicking.
- Add explicit truncation check: if the data region is shorter than
  the declared page count requires, return a descriptive DatabaseCorrupt
  error instead of panicking on out-of-bounds slice access.

Cargo dependency additions:
- Add lru = "0.12" to workspace Cargo.toml and fsqlite-core/Cargo.toml
- Cargo.lock updated accordingly (lru 0.12.5 + hashbrown transitive dep
  gains allocator-api2 and equivalent features)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -810,6 +810,7 @@ dependencies = [
  "fsqlite-vfs",
  "fsqlite-wal",
  "hashbrown 0.14.5",
+ "lru 0.12.5",
  "proptest",
  "rusqlite",
  "serde",
@@ -1262,7 +1263,7 @@ dependencies = [
  "ftui-layout",
  "ftui-render",
  "ftui-style",
- "lru",
+ "lru 0.16.3",
  "ropey",
  "rustc-hash",
  "smallvec",
@@ -1388,6 +1389,8 @@ version = "0.15.5"
 source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
 checksum = "9229cfe53dfd69f0609a49f65461bd93001ea1ef889cd5529dd176593f5338a1"
 dependencies = [
+ "allocator-api2",
+ "equivalent",
  "foldhash 0.1.5",
 ]
 
@@ -1731,6 +1734,15 @@ dependencies = [
  "tracing-subscriber",
 ]
 
+[[package]]
+name = "lru"
+version = "0.12.5"
+source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
+checksum = "234cf4f4a04dc1f57e24b96cc0cd600cf2af460d4161ac5ecdd0af8e1f3b2a38"
+dependencies = [
+ "hashbrown 0.15.5",
+]
+
 [[package]]
 name = "lru"
 version = "0.16.3"
